text: ASSESSMENT (May 11): Iran's principal strategic objective in negotiations is recognition of Hormuz sovereignty as a future deterrent — since its missile/drone/proxy deterrence proved unable to deter major US or Israeli attack. Iran is using Hormuz as leverage in all future negotiations. CSIS assesses talks as 'structurally deadlocked' unless a third-party bridge proposal emerges.
verificationNote: Critical Threats Project Iran War Update published May 11. CSIS analysis confirmed via their website.

text: SANCTIONS: US sanctioned 12 entities/individuals for Iranian oil sales to China — 3 Iran-based, 9 in Hong Kong/UAE/Oman — under 'Economic Fury.' Move timed to pressure Beijing ahead of Trump-Xi summit May 14-15. China's April crude imports fell 20% year-on-year to lowest in nearly 4 years. Saudi Aramco CEO: market losing ~100 million barrels of supply per week.
verificationNote: Bloomberg confirmed US sanctions on 12 entities. Aramco CEO remarks confirmed by CNBC. China import data from Bloomberg.

text: IRON DOME HIT: Hezbollah released FPV drone footage of a fiber-optic guided drone striking an Iron Dome launcher battery at Jal al-Alam military site in northern Israel. IDF confirmed the hit — admitted it has no reliable countermeasure to fiber-optic drone threat. This marks the first confirmed operational Iron Dome battery destruction in the conflict. IDF reservist WO Glovanyov (47) killed by separate Hezbollah explosive drone near Manara — 5th soldier killed during ceasefire.
verificationNote: Times of Israel and CNN confirmed Iron Dome hit and IDF acknowledgment. Times of Israel confirmed WO Glovanyov's death.

text: CEASEFIRE STATUS: Trump declared the Iran ceasefire on 'massive life support' after rejecting Tehran's counter-proposal as 'totally unacceptable.' NSC convened with Vance, Rubio, Hegseth, Gen. Caine, and CIA Director Ratcliffe. Options discussed: resuming Project Freedom, resuming bombing of remaining 25% of Iranian targets, and a special forces operation to seize Iran's enriched uranium stockpile.
verificationNote: Axios confirmed NSC meeting attendees and options discussed. CNN and PBS confirmed Trump's 'life support' quote verbatim.

text: IRAN THREAT: Iran's Army spokesperson Brig. Gen. Mohammad Akraminia warned of 'surprising options' if adversaries make another 'miscalculation,' threatening to take the conflict to areas 'the enemy has not anticipated.' Statement came same day as Trump's 'life support' declaration and 4th round of Oman talks described as 'difficult but constructive.'
verificationNote: Iran International and CNBC confirmed Iranian Army spokesperson statement.

text: NEGOTIATIONS: 4th round of US-Iran talks held in Oman May 11 — 'difficult but constructive.' Iran proposed joint nuclear-enrichment project with Arab states as alternative to dismantling program; US envoy Witkoff rejected this. Iran's Ghalibaf: 'They will be surprised.' EU's Kallas: political agreement reached to expand Iran sanctions to cover Hormuz navigation violations. Araghchi: US making 'unreasonable demands.'
verificationNote: CNN liveblog confirmed Oman talks. Iran International confirmed EU sanctions expansion. Al Jazeera confirmed Araghchi statement.

text: LEBANON: 51 killed in Israeli strikes in 24 hours May 10-11 — including 2 medical workers in Tibnin and Qalawiya health facilities. Lebanon death toll approaches 3,000 since March 2. Hezbollah reported 24 attack sorties on Israeli positions. IDF struck 85+ Hezbollah sites in response to drone attacks. Ceasefire exists in name only — Netanyahu: 'war is not over.'
verificationNote: Al Jazeera confirmed 51 killed including medical workers. Lebanon Health Ministry official figures. CBS News confirmed Netanyahu 60 Minutes quote.
